---
title: "Project 1 Report"
author: "Tim Cook, Alex Poplawski, and T.J. Seislove"
date: "3/25/2026"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

### Data Import
```{r}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)

Real_World_Fuel_Efficiency <- read_csv("Real-World_Fuel_Efficiency.csv") %>%
  janitor::clean_names()
```
Our dataset was stored into a dataframe titled, "Real_World_Fuel_Efficiency," which was imported from the CSV file, "Real-World_Fuel_Efficiency.csv" using the read_csv() command from the readr package. We then applied the clean_names() command from the janitor package to convert the variable names to a more consistent, friendlier format with underscores to represent spaces. Doing this allows for an easier analysis process and reduces the likelihood of potential syntax errors in our future work.
